entities.name: Careerspan
entities.context: V's talent matching company whose operational model enforces role separation

entities.name: Advocacy vs. Evaluation conflict
entities.context: Psychological dynamic where a single person managing both sides causes candidates to perform rather than disclose honestly

entities.name: Dual agency protections
entities.context: Legal/real estate principle preventing conflicts of interest through role separation

entities.name: Candidate-employer separation principle
entities.context: Core belief that candidate-facing and employer-facing representatives must be different people to ensure honest information flow

entities.name: Trusted advisor role
entities.context: The candidate-facing representative who acts as ally/coach

entities.name: Evaluator/broker role
entities.context: The employer-facing representative who maintains objectivity

relationships.context: Operational model explicitly prevents same team member from owning both candidate and employer relationships for a placement
The separation principle directly addresses this psychological conflict
Mirrors legal/real estate protections against conflicts of interest
One half of the role split - handles candidate relationship
Other half of the role split - handles employer relationship
